guardrails.title: Use Eloquent or Query Builder
guardrails.body: When using `DB::raw()` or raw SQL, parameter bindings are required. String concatenation in queries is prohibited.

guardrails.title: No Business Logic in Controllers
guardrails.body: Business logic shall be separated into Service or Action classes. Controllers shall remain thin.

guardrails.title: Use Form Request Validation
guardrails.body: Input validation shall use FormRequest classes instead of inline validation in controllers.

guardrails.title: No Unguarded Mass Assignment
guardrails.body: Models shall define `$guarded` or `$fillable`. Using `Model::unguard()` is prohibited. When columns are added or modified in migrations, verify that the corresponding model's `$fillable` or `$guarded` is updated accordingly.

guardrails.title: Eager Loading Strategy Required
guardrails.body: Features that use relations shall document the eager loading policy using Eloquent's with()/load() in the spec.

guardrails.title: Enable Lazy Loading Detection in Development
guardrails.body: Enable Model::preventLazyLoading() in AppServiceProvider for the development environment. Disable it in production and use logging instead.

guardrails.title: Use Query Scopes for Reusable Conditions
guardrails.body: Query conditions used in two or more places shall be defined as Eloquent local scopes. Duplicating WHERE clauses is prohibited.

guardrails.title: Use Enum Casting in Eloquent
guardrails.body: Enum-typed attributes shall use Eloquent casts to specify the Enum. Place Enums under app/Enums and declare the type explicitly in the model's $casts.

guardrails.title: Use Factory for Test Data
guardrails.body: Creating test data directly with Model::create() or DB::insert() in test code is prohibited. Use Factories and override only the minimum necessary attributes.

guardrails.title: Invokable Controller for Single Action
guardrails.body: Endpoints that perform a single operation outside of CRUD shall be implemented as an Invokable Controller with an __invoke() method.
